BRIEFING_SUMMARY:
Primary read: Three high-impact catalysts cluster early week — USD inflation + EUR policy tone dominate; gold and US indices most sensitive.
Operational meaning: Position size at 50% of normal risk for the catalyst window; widen exits ~30%; cancel marginal setups.
Key markets: DXY · EURUSD · XAUUSD · US500
Current risk: 🟠🟠🟠🟠⚫ High — clustered catalyst exposure — reactivity elevated through the catalyst window; mean-reversion expected thereafter.
EVENT_DAY_REFERENCE:
Event: CPI (USD)
Time (UTC): 12:30 AWST · 04:30 UTC
Expected duration: 60–120 minutes of high reactivity, then mean-reversion through next session
What to watch: First 5-min close in the surprise direction; then 15-min close as the real trend signal. Cross-confirm against DXY and the lead pair (EURUSD).
Chart study: 1H structure + 5M / 15M execution
FOUR_WAY_HIGHER:
IF surprise HIGHER
Behaviour: USD bid, indices defensive, gold pressured intraday — first 30 min sees the cleanest directional move (event: CPI (USD))
Affected markets: DXY · EURUSD · GBPUSD · USDJPY · XAUUSD · US500 · NAS100
Trader action — operationally anchored:
  Instrument: EURUSD
  Price level: pre-event reaction band around 1.0928 (lower side)
  What this means: After CPI (USD), only consider downside EURUSD continuation IF price fails to reclaim the pre-event reaction band around 1.0928. If EURUSD closes below 1.0928 and the next candle stays below it, the dollar-strength move is structurally real rather than a 60-second sweep.
  What confirms continuation:
    - 5-minute candle closes below 1.0928
    - next 5-minute candle fails to recover back above 1.0928
    - DXY continues making higher highs through the same window
  What invalidates the directional idea:
    - EURUSD closes back above 1.0928
    - candle bodies start shrinking — momentum exhausting
    - DXY loses momentum immediately after the announcement spike
  Most probable next path if it confirms:
    - price rotates toward the next liquidity zone beneath the event low (illustrative ~1.0880)
    - volatility stays elevated through the next macro catalyst window
  Most probable failure path:
    - event spike fades inside the first 10 minutes
    - EURUSD snaps back into the pre-event range
    - trapped short sellers forced to cover
Dollar impact: Long EURUSD: $500 – $1,500 drawdown on $100k notional. Short EURUSD: $500 – $1,500 gain on $100k.
FOUR_WAY_LOWER:
IF surprise LOWER
Behaviour: USD offered, indices bid, gold rallies on softening rate-path expectations — risk-on rotation (event: CPI (USD))
Affected markets: DXY · EURUSD · GBPUSD · US500 · NAS100 · XAUUSD
Trader action — operationally anchored:
  Instrument: EURUSD
  Price level: pre-event reaction band around 1.0928 (upper side)
  What this means: After CPI (USD), only consider upside EURUSD continuation IF price holds above the pre-event reaction band around 1.0928. If EURUSD closes above 1.0928 and the next candle stays above it, the dollar-weakness move is structurally real rather than a 60-second sweep.
  What confirms continuation:
    - 5-minute candle closes above 1.0928
    - next 5-minute candle holds above 1.0928 without retracing back inside
    - DXY continues making lower lows through the same window
  What invalidates the directional idea:
    - EURUSD closes back below 1.0928
    - candle bodies start shrinking — momentum exhausting
    - DXY loses downside momentum immediately after the announcement spike
  Most probable next path if it confirms:
    - price rotates toward the next liquidity zone above the event high (illustrative ~1.0980)
    - gold (XAUUSD) rallies in sympathy through the same window
  Most probable failure path:
    - dovish spike fades inside the first 10 minutes
    - EURUSD snaps back into the pre-event range
    - trapped long buyers forced to liquidate
Dollar impact: Long EURUSD: $500 – $1,500 gain on $100k notional. Short XAUUSD: $500 – $1,500 drawdown on 100 oz notional exposure.
FOUR_WAY_INLINE:
IF in-line
Behaviour: Light reaction across the board; initial spike either side then settle. Volatility drops within 15 minutes (event: CPI (USD))
Affected markets: DXY · EURUSD · XAUUSD · US500
Trader action — operationally anchored:
  Instrument: EURUSD
  Price level: pre-event reaction band around 1.0928 (range intact)
  What this means: An in-line print leaves the pre-event reaction band intact. Price will chop around 1.0928 but no side has structural control until a 15-min close breaks the band.
  What confirms continuation:
    - both 5-min candles after the print stay between roughly 1.0915 and 1.0940
    - DXY mean-reverts back toward pre-event level inside 15 minutes
    - volatility (VIX-proxy) drops back to pre-event readings
  What invalidates the directional idea:
    - a 5-min candle closes outside the 1.0915–1.0940 reaction band
    - DXY pushes to a fresh 15-min extreme in either direction
    - volume comes IN on a directional candle instead of fading
  Most probable next path if it confirms:
    - range-bound chop through the next half-hour into the following catalyst
    - the real directional bias prints from the next catalyst, not from CPI (USD)
  Most probable failure path:
    - the in-line print masks a delayed reaction — the real direction prints 10–15 minutes after the announcement on a sweep through 1.0928
    - traders standing aside catch the move on the next ATLAS scan
Dollar impact: Small swings $100 – $300 on $100k EURUSD, then mean-reversion back toward pre-event level.
FOUR_WAY_REVERSAL:
IF initial-direction reversal
Behaviour: First move off the announcement is faded inside 10 minutes; the 15-min close is the real trend (event: CPI (USD))
Affected markets: DXY · EURUSD · XAUUSD
Trader action — operationally anchored:
  Instrument: EURUSD
  Price level: pre-event reaction band 1.0928 (15-min close direction is the real trend)
  What this means: The first move off the announcement is being faded by larger volume on the other side. Trade only the post-15-min direction IF the 15-min candle closes through 1.0928 in the OPPOSITE direction of the initial spike.
  What confirms continuation:
    - 15-min candle closes opposite to the first 5-min move
    - volume IN-flow visible against the initial direction
    - DXY mirrors the reversal on the same 15-min close
  What invalidates the directional idea:
    - the 15-min close confirms the initial direction (no reversal)
    - candle body of the 15-min close is small relative to its wick
    - DXY continues in the initial-spike direction without retracing
  Most probable next path if it confirms:
    - price extends in the post-15-min direction toward the opposite-side liquidity zone (~1.0880 for a hawkish-reversal, ~1.0980 for a dovish-reversal)
    - volatility stays elevated through the next macro catalyst window
  Most probable failure path:
    - the reversal candle itself gets faded inside the next 15 minutes
    - price returns to the pre-event range
    - trapped reversal traders forced to exit on the next ATLAS scan
Dollar impact: Traders who chase the first 60 seconds: $800 – $1,500 drawdown on $100k EURUSD before the reversal completes.
MARKET_IMPACT:
Market impact: cause: CPI (USD) → expectation: front-end rate-path repricing → market reaction: USD lead, cross-asset cascade through DXY, EURUSD, XAUUSD, US500
Price reaction path: Initial impulse (0–60s) → first 5-min close confirms direction → 15-min close gives the real trend → 1H close locks in the bias for the rest of the session.
Liquidity effect: Spreads widen 2–4× across the announcement candle; market-depth thins through T+5; normalises by T+15.
Volatility effect: ATR doubles on the announcement candle; expect overshoot beyond historical 1-σ
Trader consequence: Chasing the first 60 seconds creates the $800–$1,500 drawdown band; waiting for the 5-min close and trading the next pullback flips the same setup into a clean $300–$800 gain on $100k EURUSD.
RISK_ESCALATION_HEALTHY: PRE-EVENT (T-60 → T-15): normal flow, positions sized to current mood. Optional pre-position only if structure already validated by Dark Horse scan.
RISK_ESCALATION_CAUTION: T-15 → T-0: tighten stops on existing exposure; do not add new direction trades. Sit on hands for the announcement.
RISK_ESCALATION_DANGER: T+0 → T+5: first-candle spike. Do not enter inside this window — chase risk is at session peak. Watch only.
RISK_ESCALATION_INVALIDATION: STAND-ASIDE: if the first 5-min close is opposite to your existing position direction, exit immediately at next 1-min close. RE-ENTRY: only after a confirmed 15-min close in the structural direction following CPI (USD).
WHAT_TO_DO_NOW:
1. Reduce dollar exposure to 50% of normal risk for the next 6 hours.
   WHY: Catalyst-driven sessions widen spreads and stop ranges. Smaller dollar exposure keeps planned-risk discipline through the volatility expansion.
   IF IGNORED: A normal-size position takes ~30–50% more drawdown through the announcement candle than the planned-risk model assumes — turning a designed $500 loss into a $650–$750 actual loss.
   CONFIRMATION: Position-size calculator reflects the reduced dollar exposure before the event window opens. If normal risk per trade is $500 on a $10k account, the new ceiling is ~$250.
   ACTION CHANGES WHEN: Market mood drops from HIGH back to LOW within the 6-hour window — at that point dollar exposure can scale back up gradually, ONE position at a time.
   $$: Reducing to ~$250 per trade caps the announcement-window drawdown at the planned-risk line; oversized exposure regularly turns a $500 designed loss into $700–$1,000 of realised damage.
2. Cancel marginal setups. Only act on triggers where the next candle closes BEYOND the level (confirmed directional structure test).
   WHY: Sweeps and false breaks dominate the first 15 minutes after a high-impact print; trading on a wick alone is paying for false direction.
   IF IGNORED: Acting on the first 60 seconds typically catches the wick high or low; the $300–$800 drawdown band on $100k EURUSD is paid for the privilege.
   CONFIRMATION: No new entries until the 5-minute candle CLOSES beyond the structural level AND the next candle holds the level as new support/resistance.
   ACTION CHANGES WHEN: Two consecutive 5-min closes confirm direction AND the lead pair shows momentum agreement on the trigger timeframe.
   $$: Avoids the $300–$800 drawdown band typical of chasing a first-move fake; the same setup post-confirmation pays $300–$800 in agreement direction.
3. Widen exit-points by ~30% on existing positions; tight exits get hit before direction confirms.
   WHY: Initial volatility expansion pushes price beyond historical 1-σ before the real direction prints — tight exits convert noise into realised loss.
   IF IGNORED: Tight exits get swept by the announcement-candle wick; the position is closed at a loss right before the structural direction confirms — the worst possible exit point.
   CONFIRMATION: All open exits sit at least 1.3× the pre-event ATR distance away from current price by 5 minutes before the announcement.
   ACTION CHANGES WHEN: The 15-min candle close prints the real direction; tighter exits can be reapplied 30 minutes after confirmation if the move is mean-reverting.
   $$: Avoids being stopped on a wick that mean-reverts within 5 minutes — protects $150–$400 of carry on $100k notional through the event window.
CONFIRMS_WHEN: First 5-min close in the surprise direction PLUS confirming structure on DXY / lead pair.
CANCELS_WHEN: First 15-min close fails to follow through OR geopolitical override hits the wire inside the window.

  - EURUSD upside liquidity ≈ 1.0980

AFFECTED_MARKETS_EXPANDED:
DXY
  HOW: Dollar-index basket; DIRECT primary indicator of USD repricing — leads every dollar pair.
  STRONGER-THAN-EXPECTED: DXY pushes to fresh 24-hour high on hawkish USD surprise; pairs follow.
  WEAKER-THAN-EXPECTED: DXY drops below pre-event range on dovish print; pairs rally in sympathy.
  CONFIRMATION: 15-min close beyond the pre-event range.
  INVALIDATION: 15-min close back inside the pre-event range; whipsaw without follow-through.
  KEY PRICE LEVELS: Pre-event range top/bottom is the directional gate. Above range = dollar real, below = dollar fade.
  RISK NOTE: Leads every dollar pair by 5–10 minutes — read DXY first, then act on the pair.

EURUSD
  HOW: Dollar leg of the EUR-USD pair; reacts to USD rate-path repricing first, EUR fundamentals second.
  STRONGER-THAN-EXPECTED: EURUSD drops on stronger USD print — first 30 min sees the cleanest move.
  WEAKER-THAN-EXPECTED: EURUSD rallies on softer USD print — EUR-side optimism amplifies the move.
  CONFIRMATION: 5-min candle close in the surprise direction PLUS DXY making higher highs / lower lows in agreement.
  INVALIDATION: Body close back through the pre-event reaction band (≈1.0928) within 15 minutes.
  KEY PRICE LEVELS: Reaction band 1.0928 · downside liquidity ≈1.0880 · upside liquidity ≈1.0980 · 30-day ATR ≈70 pips.
  RISK NOTE: $300–$800 swing on $100k notional in the first 30 minutes; up to $1,500 on a reversal chase.

XAUUSD
  HOW: Inverse to real-yields and USD; reacts secondary to USD prints, primary to risk-off flow.
  STRONGER-THAN-EXPECTED: XAUUSD drops intraday on a hawkish USD surprise; safe-haven flow can recover the move on a 1H close.
  WEAKER-THAN-EXPECTED: XAUUSD rallies on a dovish USD print; real-yield-fall amplifies.
  CONFIRMATION: DXY direction in agreement + 1H close beyond the structural level.
  INVALIDATION: Body close back through the pre-event range; safe-haven flow flipping the move on the 1H.
  KEY PRICE LEVELS: Structural support 2380 · structural resistance 2440 · 30-day ATR ≈$45.
  RISK NOTE: $500–$1,500 swing on 100 oz exposure in the first 30 minutes.

US500
  HOW: Equity index repricing rate-path expectations; reacts to bond yields first, dollar second.
  STRONGER-THAN-EXPECTED: US500 drops on rate-hike fears after a hawkish USD print.
  WEAKER-THAN-EXPECTED: US500 rallies on rate-cut optimism after a dovish USD print.
  CONFIRMATION: 5-min close + VIX direction (inverse) + yields in agreement.
  INVALIDATION: Body close back into the pre-event range OR VIX collapsing without index direction following.
  KEY PRICE LEVELS: VWAP-driven; first 15-min VWAP becomes the intraday pivot.
  RISK NOTE: $400–$900 swing on 100-share exposure in the first 30 minutes.
PRICE_MAP:
EURUSD — 1.0928 (reaction_level)
  WHY MATTERS: Post-event reaction band. ATLAS treats EURUSD direction as structurally real ONLY if price holds outside this band on the 5-min close.
  IF IT HOLDS: Continuation in the breakout direction toward the adjacent liquidity zone (1.0880 downside or 1.0980 upside).
  IF IT FAILS: Event fade — EURUSD returns into the pre-event range; trapped traders forced to cover.
  CONFIRMATION: 5-min close beyond the band + next candle stays beyond.
  INVALIDATION: Body close back inside the band on the next candle.
  $$ CONSEQUENCE: Failed continuation can reverse $300–$800 per $100k EURUSD notional in the first 15 minutes after invalidation.

EURUSD — 1.0880 (liquidity_pool)
  WHY MATTERS: Downside liquidity below the event low; absorbs continuation flow on a confirmed hawkish print.
  IF IT HOLDS: Downside extension exhausts here; mean-reversion attempt toward 1.0928.
  IF IT FAILS: Deeper continuation toward 1.0850; volatility stays elevated through the next macro window.
  CONFIRMATION: 1H close below 1.0880 + DXY making higher highs.
  INVALIDATION: 5-min reclaim back above 1.0880 inside 15 minutes.
  $$ CONSEQUENCE: Holding through 1.0880 → 1.0850 adds $300–$500 per $100k notional.

EURUSD — 1.0980 (liquidity_pool)
  WHY MATTERS: Upside liquidity above the event high; absorbs continuation flow on a confirmed dovish print.
  IF IT HOLDS: Upside extension exhausts here; mean-reversion attempt back toward 1.0928.
  IF IT FAILS: Deeper continuation toward 1.1010; risk-on flow stays in equities and gold.
  CONFIRMATION: 1H close above 1.0980 + DXY making lower lows.
  INVALIDATION: 5-min retracement back below 1.0980 inside 15 minutes.
  $$ CONSEQUENCE: Holding through 1.0980 → 1.1010 adds $300–$500 per $100k notional.

DXY — pre-event range (continuation_gate)
  WHY MATTERS: Dollar-index gate. DXY closing through the pre-event range tells ATLAS the dollar move is real BEFORE any individual pair confirms.
  IF IT HOLDS: Range respected → no directional dollar bias; pairs chop until the next catalyst.
  IF IT FAILS: Range broken → dollar lead in agreement direction; every dollar pair confirms within 5–10 minutes.
  CONFIRMATION: 15-min close beyond the range on broad-based dollar flow.
  INVALIDATION: 15-min close back inside the range within 30 minutes.
  $$ CONSEQUENCE: Reading DXY first protects $200–$500 per $100k notional vs. reading the pair direction in isolation.

XAUUSD — 2380 (support_zone)
  WHY MATTERS: Structural support beneath current price. Gold below 2380 on a 1H close flips the macro bias bearish on the metal complex.
  IF IT HOLDS: Mean-reversion back toward 2410; safe-haven flow returns if equity risk-off prints.
  IF IT FAILS: Deeper liquidation toward 2350; correlation with real-yields tightens.
  CONFIRMATION: 1H close above 2380 after testing it.
  INVALIDATION: 1H close below 2380.
  $$ CONSEQUENCE: Holding 100 oz exposure through a 2380 break: $300–$500 of unrealised loss before invalidation triggers.

XAUUSD — 2440 (resistance_zone)
  WHY MATTERS: Structural resistance above current price. Gold above 2440 on a 1H close flips macro bias bullish on the metal complex.
  IF IT HOLDS: Mean-reversion back toward 2410; consolidation continues.
  IF IT FAILS: Breakout toward fresh highs; real-yields fall, dollar weakness compounds.
  CONFIRMATION: 1H close above 2440 after testing it.
  INVALIDATION: 1H close back below 2440 inside 4 hours.
  $$ CONSEQUENCE: Riding 100 oz exposure through 2440: each $10 move = $1,000 of P/L.
OPERATIONAL_NARRATIVE:
Current phase: pre-event window (T-60 → T-0) into CPI (USD)
What the market is doing: Liquidity thinning; pre-event positioning visible in front-end yields and DXY; equity desks de-grossing before the announcement candle.
Why it is doing it: Market participants reprice the rate-path BEFORE the catalyst lands; once the announcement prints, the move is the difference between consensus and actual.
What changes next: At T-0 the announcement prints; the 5-min close at T+5 is the FIRST real read on direction; the 15-min close at T+15 is the REAL trend; the 1H close locks in the session bias.
What traders should avoid: New entries inside the announcement candle, adding to losers in the pre-event window, chasing the first 60-second move, oversizing into thin liquidity.
When conditions become safer again: After the 15-min close at T+15 confirms direction AND the 1H close at T+60 locks in the trend. Position-sizing can return to normal ~6 hours after the catalyst if no follow-on event is on the calendar.
